mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean excess returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-values exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a combined predictive R-squared below 0.05 in walk-forward validation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and positive short-term market sentiment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following INDPRO accelerations above 0.5%, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation during high-volatility macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and persistent consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of observations when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed their respective thresholds, invalidating the stagflation penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, directly supporting corporate earnings trajectories and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion, triggering immediate positive equity repricing and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output during a monetary tightening cycle signals capacity constraints and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive during periods of falling INDPRO within tightening cycles, or the negative predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.
